# Mako language reference (for tools / LLMs)
#
# https://mako-lang.com
# https://github.com/loreste/mako
# Version: 0.2.1
#
# Compile path: .mko -> C -> object (cached) -> native binary (clang).
# No GC. Memory is hold/share/arena. Prefer the surface below; dual spellings
# (func, :=, import, package) still parse but are not what docs lead with.

================================================================================
1. OVERVIEW
================================================================================

Mako is a compiled, statically typed language. Source files end in .mko.
Debug builds use -O0; release uses -O3 -flto.

Preferred surface:
  fn, let, on Type {}, pack / pull / export, hold / share / arena,
  crew / kick / join / fan, match, switch / case

Also:
  - no GC; hold moves, share is refcounted, arena is bump allocation
  - crew is structured concurrency (jobs do not outlive the crew)
  - fan runs a function over a slice across cores
  - generics (0.1.9): fns + structs + enums + bounds monomorphize; tuples/multi-return work
  - Result / Option; unused Result is a compile error
  - compound and parallel assignment; if as expression
  - stdlib is large (net, sql, crypto, http/2, …)
  - wasm32-wasip1 target exists
  - scripts/bench-gate.sh: aim is within about 1.5x of a C baseline on micros

Entry point: fn main(). Top level may declare fn, struct, enum, actor,
interface, const, extern "C", pack, pull, export, on.

================================================================================
2. INSTALLATION
================================================================================

# From release binary:
curl -fsSL https://github.com/loreste/mako/releases/latest/download/install-release.sh | bash

# From source:
make install    # installs to ~/.local/bin/mako + ~/.local/share/mako/runtime

# Verify:
mako --version    # prints "mako version mako0.2.1 <os>/<arch>"

================================================================================
3. BUILD COMMANDS
================================================================================

mako build file.mko                  # debug build (-O0)
mako build --release file.mko        # optimized (-O3 -flto)
mako build file.mko -o mybin         # custom output name
mako run file.mko                    # compile + run
mako run file.mko -- arg1 arg2       # compile + run with arguments
mako check file.mko                  # typecheck only (no binary)
mako check --json file.mko           # JSON diagnostics for tooling
mako test path/                      # run all *_test.mko files
mako test --run TestAdd path/        # filter tests by name
mako test -v path/                   # verbose test output
mako test --count 5 path/            # repeat tests N times
mako test --coverage path/           # with coverage
mako fmt file.mko                    # format to stdout
mako fmt file.mko -w                 # format in place
mako fmt file.mko -d                 # show diff
mako lint path/                      # lint checks
mako lint --identity path/           # flag dual-form spellings as style
mako bench path/                     # run bench_*.mko files
mako doc path/                       # generate API docs
mako init myapp                      # scaffold a new project
mako init mysvc --backend            # scaffold an API service
mako init myws --workspace           # scaffold a workspace
mako build --target wasm32-wasip1 file.mko -o out.wasm   # WASM target

Build flags:
  --release           Optimized build (-O3 -flto)
  --time              Print compilation timing
  -j N / MAKO_JOBS    Parallel compilation jobs
  --no-incremental    Disable incremental build cache
  --target <triple>   Cross-compile target
  --sanitize=thread   Thread sanitizer
  --sanitize=address  Address sanitizer
  --static-link       Static linking (default for musl targets)
  --emit-c            Emit generated C code

================================================================================
4. TYPES
================================================================================

4.1 Primitive Types
-------------------

  int         Platform integer (int64_t in C backend)
  int64       64-bit signed integer
  int32       32-bit signed integer
  int8        8-bit signed integer (-128..127, range-checked)
  uint64      Unsigned 64-bit integer (rejects negative on convert)
  byte        Unsigned 8-bit (element of []byte)
  float       64-bit floating point (alias: float64)
  bool        true or false
  string      UTF-8 text (byte-indexed, rune-iterable)
  void        No return value

Untyped integer literals default to int. Explicit conversion required between
integer kinds: int64(x), int32(x), int8(x), uint64(x), byte(x), float64(x).

No implicit mixing: int + int64 is a type error. Convert explicitly.

4.2 Composite Types
-------------------

  []T                 Slice (dynamic array): []int, []string, []byte, []float, []Point,
                      []Option[T], []Result[T,E], [][]T, []map[…]
  map[K]V             Hash map. Keys: int|string|float|bool|Struct|Enum.
                      Values: same, []T/[][]T, nested maps depth ≤3,
                      bags Option/Result (nests, channels), tuples
                      (incl. bag/chan fields), chan[T]. Struct/enum keys
                      hash field-wise / tag+payload. Monomorph C helpers
                      are demand-driven (only used map shapes are emitted).
  []bool / []Enum     Bool and enum slices (make/append/index)
  [][]T               Nested slices (headers of inner slices; monomorphized)
  chan[T]             Typed channel: int family, bool, float, string, named
                      struct/enum, tuples. make(chan[T], n) / chan_open[T](n)
                      accept the same T set. chan_len/chan_cap work on any chan[T].
  Option[T]           Some(value) or None
  Result[T, E]        Ok(value) or Err(error)
  (T, U)              Tuple: (int, int), (int, string), (Point, int), etc.
  Named structs       User-defined product types; ==/!= field-wise (strings by content)
  Named enums         User-defined sum types; ==/!= tag + payload

4.3 Tuples
----------

  // Tuple type annotation:
  fn divmod(a: int, b: int) -> (int, int) {
      return (a / b, a % b)
  }

  // Tuple destructuring / multi-return:
  let q, r = divmod(17, 5)

  // Tuple types in declarations:
  let pair: (int, string) = (42, "hello")

4.4 Explicit Conversions
------------------------

  let a: int = 10
  let b = int64(a)           // int -> int64
  let f = float64(a)         // int -> float
  print(string(a))           // int -> string "10"
  let buf = bytes("hi")      // string -> []byte (also: []byte("hi"))
  print(string(buf))         // []byte -> string

Compile-time range checking: int8(200) is rejected at mako check when the
argument is a constant.

================================================================================
5. VARIABLES AND BINDINGS
================================================================================

  let x = 1                  // immutable binding, type inferred
  let x: int = 1             // explicit type annotation
  let mut y = 2              // mutable binding
  y = y + 1                  // assignment (= only, never ==)
  const PORT = 8080          // compile-time constant
  const MAX = 10 * 100 + 80  // constant folding supported

Assignment is = only. Equality comparison is ==.

5.1 Compound Assignments
-------------------------

  let mut x = 10
  x += 5                     // x = x + 5
  x -= 2                     // x = x - 2
  x *= 3                     // x = x * 3
  x /= 2                     // x = x / 2

  // Increment / Decrement:
  x++                        // x = x + 1
  x--                        // x = x - 1

  // Works on struct fields and array indices:
  p.x += 1
  arr[0] += 10

5.2 Parallel Assignment
------------------------

  // Swap without temporaries:
  a, b = b, a

  // Multiple assignment from expressions:
  let a, b = divmod(17, 5)

  // Reassignment:
  let mut x = 1
  let mut y = 2
  x, y = y, x               // swap

================================================================================
6. OPERATORS
================================================================================

Comparison:   ==  !=  <  >  <=  >=
Arithmetic:   +  -  *  /  %
Compound:     +=  -=  *=  /=  ++  --
Logical:      &&  ||  !     (also keywords: and  or  not)
Bitwise:      &  |  ^  &^  <<  >>   (unary ^x = bitwise complement)
String:       +              (concatenation)
String cmp:   ==  !=         (no < > on strings)

Short-circuit: && and || do not evaluate the right side when unnecessary.

================================================================================
7. FUNCTIONS
================================================================================

  fn greet(name: string) -> string {
      return "hi " + name
  }

  fn add(a: int, b: int) -> int {
      return a + b
  }

  // void return (no -> annotation):
  fn say_hi() {
      print("hi")
  }

  // Trailing expression as return (when no explicit return in body):
  fn area(w: int, h: int) -> int {
      w * h
  }

Missing return on a non-void function is a type error.

7.1 User Generics (0.1.9)
  // Generic functions with monomorphization:
  fn identity[T](x: T) -> T { return x }
  // Bounds:
  fn f[T: Iface](x: T) -> T { return x }
  // Generic types:
  struct Pair[T] { a: T, b: T }
  enum Box[T] { Val(T), Nothing }
  // Built-in generic
  Result[T, E]               // built-in generic
  Option[T]                  // built-in generic


7.2 Lambdas / Closures
------------------------

  let double = |x| x * 2
  let ys = fan(xs, |x| x * x)

  // Block body lambda:
  let compute = fn(x: int) -> int {
      return x * x + 1
  }

  // Lambda with fan:
  let results = fan(items, fn(item) { item * 2 })

Lambda syntax: |params| { body } or |params| expr or fn(params) { body }

================================================================================
8. CONTROL FLOW
================================================================================

8.1 If / Else
-------------

  if x > 0 {
      print("positive")
  } else if x == 0 {
      print("zero")
  } else {
      print("negative")
  }

8.2 If with Initializer
-------------------------

  if let val = try_get(); val > 0 {
      print("got positive value")
  }

  if let err = validate(input); err != "" {
      print("invalid: " + err)
  }

8.3 If as Expression
---------------------

  let x = if condition { a } else { b }

  let label = if count > 1 { "items" } else { "item" }

  // Both branches must return the same type.

8.4 While Loop
--------------

  let mut i = 0
  while i < 10 {
      i = i + 1
  }

8.5 For / Range
---------------

  let s = [10, 20, 30]

  for i, v in range s {      // index + value
      print_int(i)
      print_int(v)
  }

  for i in range s { }       // indices only
  for _, v in range s { }    // values only (_ is blank)
  for range s { }            // no binders
  for i in range 3 { }       // 0..2 (integer range)
  for i in 3 { }             // legacy: same as range n
  for v in s { }             // legacy: values only

  // C-style for:
  for let mut i = 0; i < 10; i++ {
      print_int(i)
  }

  // Infinite loop:
  for {
      if done { break }
  }

  // Range over strings yields runes (Unicode code points):
  for i, r in range "cafe" {
      // i = byte offset, r = rune (int)
  }

  // Range over map:
  for k, v in range m {      // order unspecified
      print(k)
  }

  // Range over channel:
  for v in range ch {         // recv until close
      print_int(v)
  }

8.6 Switch / Case
------------------

  // Value switch:
  switch status {
      case 200:
          print("ok")
      case 404:
          print("not found")
      default:
          print("other")
  }

  // Expression-less switch (like chained if/else):
  switch {
      case x > 10:
          print("big")
      case x > 0:
          print("positive")
      default:
          print("non-positive")
  }

  // Switch with initializer:
  switch let v = compute(); v {
      case 0:
          print("zero")
      default:
          print("nonzero")
  }

8.7 Break / Continue
--------------------

  break                      // exit innermost loop
  continue                   // next iteration

  // Labeled loops:
  outer: while true {
      while true {
          break outer         // break outer loop
      }
  }

8.8 Defer
---------

  defer print("cleanup")     // runs on function exit (LIFO order)
  defer {
      // block form
      close(fd)
  }

8.9 Match
---------

  // Match on enums:
  match shape {
      Circle(r) => r * r,
      Rect(w, h) => w * h,
      Point => 0,
  }

  // Match on Option:
  match opt {
      Some(v) => v,
      None => fallback,
  }

  // Match on Result:
  match result {
      Ok(v) => v,
      Err(e) => { print(e); -1 },
  }

  // Match on integers:
  match n {
      0 => print("zero"),
      1 => print("one"),
      _ => print("other"),
  }

Match must be exhaustive on enums, Option, and Result.

================================================================================
9. STRUCTS
================================================================================

  struct Point {
      x: int
      y: int
  }

  // Exported struct (visible outside pack):
  export struct Point {
      x: int
      y: int
  }

  let mut p = Point { x: 3, y: 4 }
  print_int(p.x)
  p.x = 10

  // Positional struct literals:
  let p = Point{1, 2}         // x=1, y=2 (by field order)
  let zero = Point{}           // all fields zero-initialized

  // Nested structs:
  struct Addr { city: string  zip: int }
  struct Person { name: string  addr: Addr }
  let mut person = Person { name: "Ada", addr: Addr { city: "Paris", zip: 75001 } }
  person.addr.city = "Lyon"

  // Slice of structs:
  let mut xs = make([]Point, 0, 4)
  xs = append(xs, Point { x: 1, y: 2 })

================================================================================
10. METHODS (on Type blocks)
================================================================================

Methods are defined using `on Type { ... }` blocks:

  on Point {
      fn distance(self) -> int {
          return self.x + self.y
      }

      fn scale(self, k: int) -> int {
          return (self.x + self.y) * k
      }
  }

  // Call methods with dot syntax:
  let p = Point { x: 3, y: 4 }
  print(p.distance())
  print(p.scale(2))

  // Multiple methods in one block:
  on Circle {
      fn area(self) -> float {
          return 3.14159 * float64(self.r * self.r)
      }

      fn circumference(self) -> float {
          return 2.0 * 3.14159 * float64(self.r)
      }
  }

  // Methods desugar to Type_method free functions internally:
  // on Point { fn distance(self) -> int } becomes fn Point_distance(self: Point) -> int

  // Legacy free-function style also works:
  fn Point_translate(self: Point, dx: int, dy: int) -> Point {
      return Point { x: self.x + dx, y: self.y + dy }
  }

================================================================================
11. MODULE SYSTEM (pack / pull / export)
================================================================================

11.1 Pack Declaration
---------------------

  // At the top of a file, declare which pack this belongs to:
  pack mylib

11.2 Pull (Import)
-------------------

  // Pull a standard library package:
  pull "strings"
  pull "crypto"

  // Pull with alias:
  pull "strings" as str

  // Pull a local file:
  pull "./my_lib.mko"
  pull "./my_lib.mko" as lib

  // Grouped pull:
  pull (
      "strings"
      "path"
      lib "./my_lib.mko"
  )

  // Usage is always qualified:
  strings.contains("hello", "ell")
  lib.add(1, 2)

11.3 Export (Visibility)
-------------------------

  // By default, symbols are visible within the pack.
  // Use export to make them available to other packs:

  export fn add(a: int, b: int) -> int {
      return a + b
  }

  export struct Point {
      x: int
      y: int
  }

  export const VERSION = "1.0.0"

  // Non-exported symbols are pack-private:
  fn helper() -> int {        // only visible within this pack
      return 42
  }

11.4 Dual Forms (compat sugar)
-------------------------------

  // These parse but are not the preferred surface:
  import "strings"            // dual of: pull "strings"
  import "lib" as l           // dual of: pull "lib" as l
  package main                // dual of: pack main

================================================================================
12. ENUMS
================================================================================

  enum Shape {
      Circle(int),
      Rect(int, int),
      Point,
  }

  fn describe(s: Shape) -> int {
      match s {
          Circle(r) => r * r,
          Rect(w, h) => w * h,
          Point => 0,
      }
  }

  // Enum methods via on block:
  on Shape {
      fn area(self) -> int {
          match self {
              Circle(r) => r * r,
              Rect(w, h) => w * h,
              Point => 0,
          }
      }
  }

================================================================================
13. INTERFACES
================================================================================

  interface Writer {
      fn write(string) -> int
  }

  // Implementation via naming convention:
  fn Writer_write(s: string) -> int {
      print(s)
      return str_len(s)
  }

  // With self parameter:
  interface Adder {
      fn add(self, delta: int) -> int
  }

  fn Adder_add(self: Counter, delta: int) -> int {
      return self.val + delta
  }

  // Dynamic dispatch (interface values box concrete receivers):
  let a: Adder = my_counter    // boxes into fat pointer

================================================================================
14. GENERICS (0.1.9)

  // User-defined generic functions (monomorphized at compile time):
  fn identity[T](x: T) -> T { return x }
  fn first[T, U](a: T, b: U) -> T { return a }
  // Dual sugar: fn id<T>(x: T) -> T

  // Interface bounds (structural method set):
  fn describe_all[T: Describable](x: T) -> string { return x.describe() }

  // Generic structs / enums — type args required at construction:
  struct Pair[T] { a: T, b: T }
  enum MyBox[T] { Val(T), Nothing }
  let p = Pair[int] { a: 1, b: 2 }
  fn wrap(v: int) -> MyBox[int] { return Val(v) }

  // Built-in generic types:
  Result[T, E]
  Option[T]
  Job[T]
  List[T]
  map[K]V
  chan[T]
  []T

  // Seeds: iterator next() -> Option[T] for `for`; mutable capture cells.
  // Limitation: by-value self does not auto-advance iterators.


15. ERROR HANDLING
================================================================================

Mako uses Result[T, E] and Option[T] instead of null or exceptions.

15.1 Result Type
----------------

  fn parse(s: string) -> Result[int, string] {
      if str_len(s) == 0 {
          return error("empty input")
      }
      return Ok(42)
  }

  // The ? operator propagates errors (early return on Err):
  fn double_parse(s: string) -> Result[int, string] {
      let v = parse(s)?          // returns Err early if parse fails
      return Ok(v * 2)
  }

  // Match for explicit handling:
  match parse("hello") {
      Ok(v) => print_int(v),
      Err(e) => print(e),
  }

15.2 Error Construction
-----------------------

  error("message")                           // -> Result with Err
  errorf("missing %s", "config.toml")        // formatted error

15.3 Error Wrapping
-------------------

  let r = wrap_err(error("boom"), "parse")   // Err("parse: boom")
  let ok = wrap_err(Ok(7), "unused")         // still Ok(7)
  error_context(err_result, "context")       // alias for wrap_err

15.4 Error Utilities
--------------------

  error_is(err_result, "substring")          // bool: error contains substring
  error_string(err_result)                   // extract error message string
  error_tag("not_found", "user 42")          // enum-like string error
  error_join(r1, r2)                         // combine Results

15.5 Rules
----------

  - Unused Result as a statement is a compile error
  - Use ?, match, or let _ = ... to handle Results
  - Prefer Result + ? + wrap_err over abort for expected failures

================================================================================
16. SLICES
================================================================================

  let mut s = [1, 2, 3]                // []int
  let mut a: []int64 = [10, 20, 30]    // []int64
  let mut b: []byte = [72, 105]        // []byte
  let xs: []string = ["a", "b"]        // []string
  let fs: []float = [1.0, 2.0]         // []float

  // Pre-sized allocation:
  let s = make([]int, 3, 8)            // len 3, cap 8
  let z = make([]byte, 2)              // len 2
  let names = make([]string, 0, 4)     // len 0, cap 4

  // Operations:
  print_int(len(s))                    // length
  print_int(cap(s))                    // capacity
  s = append(s, 4)                     // append (assign result back!)
  let n = copy(dst, src)               // copy elements, returns count

  // Slicing:
  let t = s[1:3]                       // sub-slice (shares backing store)
  let u = s[:3]                        // from start
  let v = s[1:]                        // to end
  let w = s[:]                         // full slice

  // String <-> []byte conversion (copies):
  let buf = bytes("mako")              // string -> []byte
  let buf2 = []byte("mako")            // same as bytes()
  print(string(buf))                   // []byte -> string

================================================================================
17. MAPS
================================================================================

  let mut m = make(map[string]int)
  let mut m2 = make(map[string]int, 64)   // with size hint
  let mut mi = make(map[int]int)
  let mut ms = make(map[string]string)
  let mut mf = make(map[int]float)
  let mut fi = make(map[float]int)
  let mut ff = make(map[float]float)
  // Keys: int|string|float|bool|Struct|Enum (pack types too)
  // Values: same | []T | map[K2]V (depth ≤3) | Option/Result bags | tuples | chan[T]
  // struct values: map[int]Point, map[string]Point, map[float]Point, map[*]eng.Table
  // struct keys:   map[Point]int|string|float|bool|Struct, map[eng.Table]int|Point
  // bool / set:    map[string]bool, map[bool]int
  // enum:          map[Color]int, map[int]Color, map[Color]Point
  // slice values:  map[string][]int, map[Point][]string
  // nested maps:   map[string]map[string]int, map[string]map[string]map[string]int
  // bag values:    map[string]Option[int], map[int]Result[string,string]
  // mixed nests:   map[string]Option[Result[int,string]], []Option[Option[int]]
  // bag tuples:    map[string](Option[int], int)
  // channels:      map[string]chan[int], map[string]Option[chan[int]]
  // nested slices: [][]int, [][]Point (make/append/index/range)
  // monomorphs:    demand-driven — only used map[K]V shapes emit C helpers

  m["a"] = 1                           // insert
  print_int(m["a"])                    // get (missing -> zero value)
  print_int(m["missing"])              // 0 (no panic)

  if has(m, "a") { }                   // presence check

  let v, ok = m["a"]                   // comma-ok pattern
  if ok {
      print_int(v)
  }

  delete(m, "a")                       // remove
  print_int(len(m))                    // entry count (nil map -> 0)

  // Iteration (order unspecified):
  for k, v in range m {
      print(k)
      print_int(v)
  }

  // Slice values / nested maps:
  let mut groups = make(map[string][]int)
  groups["a"] = [1, 2, 3]
  let mut nested = make(map[string]map[string]int)
  let mut row = make(map[string]int)
  row["x"] = 1
  nested["a"] = row
  print_int(nested["a"]["x"])          // 1

  // Bag values (Option / Result per key):
  let mut maybe = make(map[string]Option[int])
  maybe["a"] = Some(42)
  maybe["b"] = None
  match maybe["a"] {
      Some(v) => print_int(v),
      None => {},
  }
  let mut tried = make(map[int]Result[string, string])
  tried[1] = Ok("yes")
  tried[2] = Err("no")

  // Helpers (all map kinds):
  let ks = maps_keys(m)                // []K
  let vs = maps_values(m)              // []V
  let c = maps_clone(m)                // shallow (nested: inner pointers)
  maps_equal(m, c)                     // 1 if equal (structs: structural)
  maps_copy(c, m)
  maps_clear(c)

  // Float keys: +0 and -0 are the same; all NaNs share one key.
  // Struct keys: field-wise eq + stable field hash. Enum keys: tag + payload.

================================================================================
18. STRINGS
================================================================================

  let s = "hello"
  let escaped = "hi\tthere\n"         // escapes: \n \t \r \\ \"

  // Concatenation:
  let greeting = "ma" + "ko"

  // Comparison (== and != only, no < > on strings):
  if s == "hello" { }

  // Byte operations:
  print_int(len(s))                   // byte length (5)
  print_int(int(s[0]))               // byte at index
  print(s[1:4])                       // byte substring "ell"

  // Unicode:
  print_int(rune_count("cafe"))       // rune count

  // Range yields runes:
  for i, r in range "cafe" {
      // i = byte offset, r = rune as int
  }

  // Conversion:
  print(string(42))                   // int -> string "42"
  let b = []byte("Hi!")               // string -> []byte
  print(string(b))                    // []byte -> string

================================================================================
19. OWNERSHIP MODEL
================================================================================

19.1 let / mut
--------------

  let x = 1                           // immutable
  let mut y = 2                       // mutable
  y = y + 1

19.2 hold (Move Semantics)
--------------------------

  hold let x = 7
  hold let y = x                      // x is moved to y
  // print_int(x)                     // ERROR: use of moved value

  // Move into function call:
  hold let x = 42
  print_int(id(x))                    // x moved into id()
  // print_int(x)                     // ERROR: moved

  // Mutable hold:
  hold let mut x = 7
  x = 9                               // reassign before move is OK
  print_int(x)

  // Partial moves (structs):
  hold let p = Point { x: 1, y: 2 }
  let x = p.x                         // moves only x field
  print_int(p.y)                      // y still usable
  // print_int(p.x)                   // ERROR: field moved

  // Copy types (int, float, bool, byte) can be re-read after hold:
  hold let n = 42
  let a = n                           // Copy: n still usable
  print_int(n)                        // OK for Copy types

19.3 share (Reference Counting)
-------------------------------

  hold let a = 1
  share let s = share_int(a)
  print_int(share_get(s))
  share_drop(s)                        // or auto-dropped at block end

  // share let is always immutable (share let mut is rejected)
  // Cannot mutate source while share is live
  // Atomic RC: share_set for updates

19.4 arena (Bump Allocation)
----------------------------

  arena a {
      let mut s = make([]int, 3, 8)   // allocated from arena
      s[0] = 10
      s = append(s, 4)                // grows from arena
      // All arena memory freed when block exits
  }

  // Text in arena:
  arena a {
      let label = arena_text(a, "mako")
      print(label)
  }

================================================================================
20. CONCURRENCY
================================================================================

20.1 crew Blocks (Structured Concurrency)
-----------------------------------------

Jobs cannot outlive their crew block:

  crew t {
      let p = t.kick(producer(ch, 5))     // spawn task
      let c = t.kick(consumer(ch))         // spawn task
      let _ = p.join()                     // wait for completion
      print_int(c.join())                  // join returns the result
  }

  // Cancel:
  crew t {
      t.cancel()                          // cooperative cancel
      if t.cancelled() { break }
  }

20.2 go Statement
------------------

  // Kick a function onto the enclosing crew:
  crew t {
      go expensive_work()                 // spawns onto t
      go another_task()
  }

  // Equivalent to t.kick(f()) but shorter syntax.

20.3 fan (Data-Parallel Map)
----------------------------

  let xs = [1, 2, 3, 4]
  let ys = fan(xs, fn(x) { x * x })      // parallel map across all cores

  // fan uses hardware concurrency (not a fixed thread count)
  // Works on []int, []float, []string

  for v in ys {
      print_int(v)
  }

20.4 Channels
-------------

  let ch = chan_new(4)                    // buffered channel, capacity 4
  let _ = ch.send(1)                     // send
  let v = ch.recv()                      // receive
  ch.close()                             // close channel

  // Typed channels:
  let ich = chan_open[int](8)
  let sch = chan_open[string](4)
  let bch = chan_open[bool](2)

  // Non-blocking send:
  let ok = chan_try_send(ch, 42)         // 0 on success

  // Channel info:
  print_int(chan_len(ch))                // items in channel (any chan[T])
  print_int(chan_cap(ch))                // capacity (any chan[T])

  // Range over channel:
  for v in range ch {                    // recv until close
      print_int(v)
  }

20.5 select
-----------

  select timeout 30 {
      a => { print("got a") }
      b => { print("got b") }
      default => { print("default ok") }
  }

  // Value of ready arm:
  let val = chan_select_value()

  // Helpers:
  let which = chan_select2(ch_a, ch_b, timeout_ms)
  let which = chan_select3(ch_a, ch_b, ch_c, timeout_ms)

20.6 Actors
-----------

  actor Session {
      receive Invite { print("invite") }
      receive Timer { print("tick") }
      receive Bye { print("bye") }
  }

  fn main() {
      let session = Session_spawn()
      crew t {
          let loopj = t.kick(Session_loop(session))
          let _ = Session_send(session, Session_Invite())
          let _ = Session_send(session, Session_Bye())
          print_int(loopj.join())
      }
  }

20.7 Concurrent Maps (CMap)
---------------------------

Thread-safe hash map, safe to share across crew tasks:

  let m = cmap_new()
  cmap_set(m, "hello", "world")
  print(cmap_get(m, "hello"))            // "world"
  print_int(cmap_has(m, "hello"))        // 1
  print_int(cmap_len(m))                 // 1
  let n = cmap_incr(m, "counter", 5)     // atomic increment
  print_int(cmap_del(m, "hello"))        // 1 (existed)

20.8 Sendable Types Across kick
--------------------------------

Only certain types can cross kick boundaries (Send-like seed):
- Copy types: int, float, bool, byte
- string (immutable, auto-cloned)
- deep-POD named structs (scalar/string/nested POD fields only)
- Option / Result / tuples of sendable payloads
- enums with sendable payloads (no map/array fields)
- chan handles
- CMap, Mutex, RWMutex, AtomicInt (sync handles)
- ShareInt (atomic RC)

Rejected: arrays, maps, arenas, non-POD structs (map/slice fields).
Prefer chan[Struct] for multi-field worker results (do not bit-pack into one int).

Loops: for i, v in range s / for k, v in range m / C-style for.
F-strings: f"hello {name}" (stack-based builder, zero malloc for short strings).
Format: fmt_sprintf* / fmt_sprint* for printf-style verbs.
Dispatch: match on strings/ints/enums; switch/case on values.
See docs/ERGONOMICS.md and docs/SPEED.md.

================================================================================
21. MODULE SYSTEM (pack / pull / export) — DETAILED
================================================================================

21.1 Package Declaration
-------------------------

  pack mylib                             // this file belongs to pack "mylib"

21.2 Pull (Import)
-------------------

  pull "strings"                         // standard library
  pull "crypto"
  pull "./my_lib.mko"                    // local file (flat merge)
  pull "./my_lib.mko" as lib             // namespaced

  // Grouped:
  pull (
      "strings"
      "path"
      lib "./my_lib.mko"
  )

  // Usage always qualified:
  strings.contains("hello", "ell")
  lib.add(1, 2)

21.3 Export
-----------

  export fn public_api() -> int { return 1 }
  export struct Config { port: int }
  export const VERSION = "1.0.0"

  fn internal_helper() -> int { return 42 }   // pack-private

21.4 Package Dependencies (mako.toml)
--------------------------------------

  # mako.toml
  [package]
  name = "myapp"
  version = "0.1.0"

  [dependencies]
  "helper" = { path = "../helper", version = "0.1.0" }
  "tool" = { path = "../tool", version = "0.1.0" }

  # Commands:
  mako pkg add helper ../helper
  mako pkg fetch
  mako pkg list
  mako pkg lock
  mako pkg audit

  # Usage: symbols namespaced by dependency key
  // In code: helper.add(1, 2)

21.5 Workspace
--------------

  # mako.toml (workspace root)
  [workspace]
  members = ["core", "helper", "app"]

  # Commands from workspace root:
  mako check .
  mako build .
  mako test .
  mako run -p app

================================================================================
22. TESTING
================================================================================

  // Test file: add_test.mko (same directory as code)
  // Test functions start with "Test":

  fn TestAdd() {
      assert_eq(add(2, 3), 5)
  }

  fn TestAddTable() {
      let a = [1, 2, 10]
      let b = [1, 3, 5]
      let want = [2, 5, 15]
      for i in 3 {
          assert_eq(add(a[i], b[i]), want[i])
      }
  }

  // Subtests:
  fn TestSubs() {
      t_run("case_one")
      assert_eq(1, 1)
      t_run("case_two")
      assert_eq(2, 2)
  }

  // Run tests:
  mako test path/
  mako test --run TestAdd path/
  mako test -v path/

================================================================================
23. JSON AND DERIVE
================================================================================

23.1 derive(json)
-----------------

  #[derive(json)]
  struct Person {
      name: string
      age: int
  }

  fn main() {
      let j = Person_to_json("Ada", 36)
      let name = Person_name_from_json(j)
      let age = Person_age_from_json(j)
  }

23.2 JSON Functions
-------------------

  // Construction:
  json_object("key", "value")
  json_ss("k1", "v1", "k2", "v2")
  json_si("name", "Ada", "age", 36)
  json_i("count", 42)
  json_nest("addr", addr_json)
  json_merge(obj1, obj2)

  // Extraction:
  json_get_string(json, "key")
  json_get_int(json, "key")
  json_get_object(json, "key")

  // Path navigation:
  json_path_string(json, "addr", "city")
  json_path_int(json, "data", "count")

  // Arrays:
  json_array_len(json)
  json_array_get_int(json, 0)
  json_array_get_string(json, 0)
  json_array_push_int(arr, 40)
  json_array_push_string(arr, "c")

================================================================================
24. EXTERN C (FFI)
================================================================================

  extern "C" fn mako_c_abs(n: int) -> int
  extern "C" fn mako_c_add(a: int, b: int) -> int

  fn main() {
      print_int(mako_c_abs(0 - 42))     // 42
      print_int(mako_c_add(20, 22))     // 42
  }

================================================================================
25. WASM / WASI
================================================================================

  # Build for WASI (requires wasi-sdk, set WASI_SDK_PATH):
  mako build file.mko --target wasm32-wasip1 -o out.wasm

  # Run with wasmtime:
  wasmtime out.wasm
  wasmtime --env KEY=val out.wasm arg1 arg2
  wasmtime --dir=./sandbox::. out.wasm    # filesystem access

  # Available in WASI: print, fib, argc, arg_get, env_get,
  # read_file, write_file (with --dir preopens)
  # Not available in WASI: sockets, TLS, databases

================================================================================
26. STANDARD LIBRARY REFERENCE
================================================================================

Below are the most important built-in functions organized by category.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.1 Output
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  print(s: string) -> void                    Print string to stdout
  print_int(n: int) -> void                   Print integer to stdout
  print_int64(n: int64) -> void               Print int64
  print_int32(n: int32) -> void               Print int32
  print_int8(n: int8) -> void                 Print int8
  print_uint64(n: uint64) -> void             Print uint64
  print_float(f: float) -> void               Print float
  print_bool(b: bool) -> void                 Print bool
  dbg(n: int) -> int                          Debug-print int, return it
  dbg_str(s: string) -> string                Debug-print string, return it
  format_int(n: int) -> string                Int to string
  format_float(f: float, prec: int) -> string Float to string with precision
  format_bool(b: bool) -> string              Bool to "true"/"false"
  int_to_string(n: int) -> string             Int to string
  fmt_sprintf(fmt: string, arg: string) -> string    Sprintf with string arg
  fmt_sprintf_d(fmt: string, arg: int) -> string     Sprintf with int arg

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.2 Strings
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  str_len(s: string) -> int                   Byte length
  str_eq(a: string, b: string) -> bool        Equality test
  str_contains(s: string, sub: string) -> bool  Contains substring
  str_has_prefix(s: string, pfx: string) -> bool  Starts with
  str_has_suffix(s: string, sfx: string) -> bool  Ends with
  str_index(s: string, sub: string) -> int    First occurrence index, -1 if not found
  str_last_index(s: string, sub: string) -> int  Last occurrence
  str_trim(s: string, cutset: string) -> string  Trim chars from both ends
  str_trim_space(s: string) -> string         Trim whitespace
  str_trim_left(s: string, cutset: string) -> string
  str_trim_right(s: string, cutset: string) -> string
  str_to_lower(s: string) -> string           Lowercase
  str_to_upper(s: string) -> string           Uppercase
  str_repeat(s: string, n: int) -> string     Repeat n times
  str_replace(s: string, old: string, new: string) -> string  Replace all
  str_split(s: string, sep: string) -> []string  Split by separator
  str_fields(s: string) -> []string           Split on whitespace
  str_join(parts: []string, sep: string) -> string  Join with separator
  str_count(s: string, sub: string) -> int    Count occurrences
  str_cut(s: string, sep: string) -> []string Cut at first sep -> [before, after]
  rune_count(s: string) -> int                Unicode code point count

  // String builder (growable buffer):
  str_builder() -> StrBuilder
  builder_write(sb: StrBuilder, s: string) -> void
  builder_write_byte(sb: StrBuilder, b: byte) -> void
  builder_string(sb: StrBuilder) -> string
  builder_len(sb: StrBuilder) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.3 Math
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  abs(n: int) -> int                          Absolute value (int)
  abs_f(f: float) -> float                    Absolute value (float)
  min(a: int, b: int) -> int                  Minimum
  max(a: int, b: int) -> int                  Maximum
  clamp(val: int, lo: int, hi: int) -> int    Clamp to range
  clamp_f(val: float, lo: float, hi: float) -> float
  sqrt(f: float) -> float                     Square root
  sin(f: float) -> float                      Sine (radians)
  cos(f: float) -> float                      Cosine (radians)
  atan2(y: float, x: float) -> float          Arctangent
  floor_f(f: float) -> float                  Floor
  ceil_f(f: float) -> float                   Ceiling
  lerp(a: float, b: float, t: float) -> float Linear interpolation
  dist2d(x1: float, y1: float, x2: float, y2: float) -> float  2D distance
  math_pow(base: float, exp: float) -> float  Power
  math_log(f: float) -> float                 Natural log
  math_exp(f: float) -> float                 Exponential
  rand_seed(seed: int) -> void                Seed RNG
  rand_intn(n: int) -> int                    Random int in [0, n)
  rand_float() -> float                       Random float in [0.0, 1.0)
  random_int(lo: int, hi: int) -> int         Crypto-random int in [lo, hi]
  safe_add(a: int, b: int) -> int             Overflow-checked addition
  checked_add(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Checked addition (Err on overflow)
  checked_sub(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Checked subtraction
  checked_mul(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Checked multiplication
  would_overflow_add(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if a+b would overflow, else 0
  would_overflow_sub(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if a-b would overflow, else 0
  would_overflow_mul(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if a*b would overflow, else 0

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.4 Parsing & Conversion
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  parse_int(s: string) -> Result[int, string]    Parse string as int
  parse_float(s: string) -> float                Parse string as float
  parse_bool(s: string) -> Result[int, string]   Parse string as bool (1/0)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.5 Slices & Arrays
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  len(arr: []T) -> int                        Length
  cap(arr: []T) -> int                        Capacity
  append(arr: []T, val: T) -> []T             Append, return new slice
  copy(dst: []T, src: []T) -> int             Copy elements, return count
  ints_contains(arr: []int, val: int) -> bool  Check membership
  strings_contains(arr: []string, val: string) -> bool
  ints_index(arr: []int, val: int) -> int     Index of value, -1 if missing
  ints_copy(arr: []int) -> []int              Shallow copy
  sort_ints(arr: []int) -> []int              Sort ascending
  sort_strings(arr: []string) -> []string     Sort ascending
  slices_reverse(arr: []int) -> []int         Reverse
  slices_unique(arr: []int) -> []int          Remove duplicates

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.6 Maps
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  has(m: map[K]V, key: K) -> bool             Key presence check
  delete(m: map[K]V, key: K)                  Remove entry
  maps_keys(m: map[K]V) -> []K                All keys (K = string|int|float)
  maps_values(m: map[K]V) -> []V              All values (int|string|float|Struct)
  maps_clear(m: map[K]V) -> void              Remove all
  maps_clone(m: map[K]V) -> map[K]V           Shallow copy
  maps_equal(a: map[K]V, b: map[K]V) -> int   Same keys/values (structs structural)
  maps_copy(dst: map[K]V, src: map[K]V) -> void  Copy entries into dst

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.7 File I/O
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  read_file(path: string) -> string           Read entire file
  write_file(path: string, data: string) -> int  Write/overwrite file
  append_file(path: string, data: string) -> int  Append to file
  remove_file(path: string) -> int            Delete file
  file_exists(path: string) -> bool           Check existence
  is_dir(path: string) -> bool                Check if directory
  read_dir(path: string) -> []string          List directory entries
  mkdir(path: string) -> int                  Create directory
  getcwd() -> string                          Current working directory
  chdir(path: string) -> int                  Change directory
  path_join(a: string, b: string) -> string   Join path segments
  path_base(path: string) -> string           Last element of path
  path_dir(path: string) -> string            Directory portion
  path_ext(path: string) -> string            File extension
  path_clean(path: string) -> string          Normalize path
  path_is_abs(path: string) -> bool           Check if absolute
  filepath_walk(root: string) -> []string     Recursive file listing
  embed_file(path: string) -> string          Embed file at compile time

  // Direct I/O (low-level):
  file_open(path: string, flags: int, mode: int) -> int
  file_close(fd: int) -> int
  pread(fd: int, count: int, offset: int) -> string
  pwrite(fd: int, data: string, offset: int) -> int
  fsync(fd: int) -> int
  fdatasync(fd: int) -> int
  fallocate(fd: int, size: int) -> int
  file_size(fd: int) -> int
  file_truncate(fd: int, size: int) -> int

  // Memory-mapped files:
  mmap_open(path: string, size: int) -> MMap
  mmap_create(path: string, size: int) -> MMap
  mmap_read(m: MMap, offset: int, len: int) -> string
  mmap_write(m: MMap, offset: int, data: string) -> int
  mmap_sync(m: MMap, flags: int) -> int
  mmap_size(m: MMap) -> int
  mmap_close(m: MMap) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.8 Environment & CLI
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  env_get(key: string) -> string              Get env var
  env_set(key: string, val: string) -> int    Set env var
  env_get_or(key: string, fallback: string) -> string  Get with default
  env_has(key: string) -> int                 Check if set
  argc() -> int                               Argument count
  args() -> []string                          All arguments
  arg_get(idx: int) -> string                 Argument by index
  flag_string(name: string, default: string) -> string  Parse string flag
  flag_int(name: string, default: int) -> int Parse int flag
  flag_bool(name: string, default: int) -> int Parse bool flag

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.9 Time
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  now_ns() -> int                             Nanoseconds since epoch
  now_ms() -> int                             Milliseconds since epoch
  time_unix() -> int                          Unix timestamp (seconds)
  time_format(unix: int) -> string            Format as RFC3339 UTC
  sleep_ms(ms: int) -> void                   Sleep milliseconds
  time_sleep_ms(ms: int) -> void              Alias for sleep_ms
  elapsed_ms(start: int) -> int               Ms elapsed since start

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.10 HTTP Server
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  http_bind(port: int) -> int                 Bind listener on port
  http_accept(listener: int) -> int           Accept connection -> conn id
  http_method(conn: int) -> string            Request method
  http_path(conn: int) -> string              Request path
  http_body(conn: int) -> string              Request body
  http_header(conn: int, name: string) -> string  Request header
  http_respond(conn: int, status: int, body: string) -> int
  http_respond_json(conn: int, status: int, json: string) -> int
  http_respond_ct(conn: int, status: int, body: string, ct: string) -> int
  http_close(conn: int) -> int                Close connection
  http_close_listener(listener: int) -> int   Close listener
  http_next(conn: int) -> int                 Next request (keep-alive)
  http_keepalive(conn: int) -> int            Check keep-alive support

  // Request parsing:
  http_request_parse(raw: string) -> HttpRequest
  http_route_match(req: HttpRequest, method: string, pattern: string) -> bool
  http_route_param(req: HttpRequest, pattern: string, name: string) -> string

  // Graceful shutdown:
  http_shutdown_begin(timeout: int) -> int
  http_shutdown_requested() -> int
  http_shutdown_ready() -> int

  // HTTP/2
  // http2_data_frame splits payloads over 16384 bytes into multiple DATA frames
  // (END_STREAM only on the last). TLS reply helpers do the same. Oversized
  // single frames make browsers fail with ERR_HTTP2_FRAME_SIZE_ERROR.
  http2_conn_new() -> Http2Conn
  http2_use(conn: Http2Conn, fd: int) -> int
  http2_response(conn: Http2Conn, stream: int, status: int, body: string) -> int
  http2_free(conn: Http2Conn) -> int
  http_forward(upstream: string, req: HttpRequest) -> string
  http2_detect(data: string) -> bool
  http2_headers_frame(stream: int, block: string, flags: int) -> string
  http2_data_frame(stream: int, payload: string, flags: int) -> string
  http2_continuation_frame(stream: int, block: string, flags: int) -> string
  http2_goaway_frame(last_stream: int, error_code: int) -> string
  http2_ping_frame(data: string, ack: int) -> string
  http2_window_update_frame(stream: int, increment: int) -> string
  http2_rst_stream_frame(stream: int, error_code: int) -> string
  http2_priority_frame(stream: int, dep: int, weight: int, exclusive: int) -> string
  http2_push_promise_frame(stream: int, promised: int, block: string, flags: int) -> string
  http2_client_preface() -> string
  http2_server_preface() -> string
  http2_empty_settings() -> string
  http2_settings_ack() -> string
  http2_settings_max_concurrent(max: int) -> string
  http2_frame_type(frame: string) -> int
  http2_frame_stream(frame: string) -> int
  http2_frame_len(frame: string) -> int
  http2_frame_flags(frame: string) -> int
  http2_frame_payload(frame: string) -> string
  http2_frame_at(data: string, idx: int) -> string
  http2_concat_frames(a: string, b: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.11 HTTP Client
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  http_get(url: string) -> string             GET request
  http_post(url: string, body: string) -> string  POST request
  http_request(method: string, url: string, body: string, timeout: int) -> string
  http_get_timeout(url: string, ms: int) -> string
  http_post_timeout(url: string, body: string, ms: int) -> string
  http_last_status() -> int                   Status of last response
  http_last_header(name: string) -> string    Header from last response

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.12 JSON (see also section 23)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  json_object(key: string, value: string) -> string
  json_get_string(json: string, key: string) -> string
  json_get_int(json: string, key: string) -> int
  json_get_object(json: string, key: string) -> string
  json_has(json: string, key: string, value: string) -> int
  json_nest(key: string, inner: string) -> string
  json_merge(a: string, b: string) -> string
  json_ss(k1: string, v1: string, k2: string, v2: string) -> string
  json_si(k1: string, v1: string, k2: string, v2: int) -> string
  json_i(key: string, value: int) -> string
  json_object_from_map_ss(m: map[string]string) -> string
  json_path_string(json: string, path: string, key: string) -> string
  json_path_int(json: string, path: string, key: string) -> int
  json_array_len(json: string) -> int
  json_array_get_int(json: string, idx: int) -> int
  json_array_get_string(json: string, idx: int) -> string
  json_array_push_string(json: string, val: string) -> string
  json_array_push_int(json: string, val: int) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.13 Database
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Unified SQL (SQLite + PostgreSQL):
  sql_open_sqlite(path: string) -> SqlDB
  sql_open_postgres(connstr: string) -> SqlDB
  sql_ok(db: SqlDB) -> int
  sql_close(db: SqlDB) -> int
  sql_exec(db: SqlDB, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_exec_plain(db: SqlDB, sql: string) -> int
  sql_exec_str4(db: SqlDB, sql: string, a: string, b: string, c: string, d: string) -> int
  sql_query_int(db: SqlDB, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_query_str(db: SqlDB, sql: string, param: string) -> string
  sql_begin(db: SqlDB) -> int
  sql_commit(db: SqlDB) -> int
  sql_rollback(db: SqlDB) -> int
  sql_prepare(db: SqlDB, sql: string) -> int
  sql_migrate(db: SqlDB, version: int, sql: string) -> int

  // Connection pool:
  sql_pool_open_sqlite(path: string, size: int) -> int
  sql_pool_open_postgres(connstr: string, size: int) -> int
  sql_pool_query_int(pool: int, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_pool_exec(pool: int, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_pool_close(pool: int) -> int

  // PostgreSQL (libpq):
  pg_connect(connstr: string) -> PgConn
  pg_exec(conn: PgConn, sql: string) -> int
  pg_close(conn: PgConn) -> int

  // MySQL:
  mysql_connect(connstr: string) -> MysqlConn
  mysql_ok(conn: MysqlConn) -> int
  mysql_close(conn: MysqlConn) -> int

  // Redis:
  redis_connect(addr: string) -> RedisConn
  redis_conn_set(conn: RedisConn, key: string, val: string) -> string
  redis_conn_get(conn: RedisConn, key: string) -> string
  redis_conn_del(conn: RedisConn, key: string) -> string
  redis_close(conn: RedisConn) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.14 Crypto & Security
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  sha1(data: string) -> string               SHA-1 hash (raw bytes)
  sha256(data: string) -> string             SHA-256 hash (raw bytes)
  sha512(data: string) -> string             SHA-512 hash (raw bytes)
  hmac_sha256(key: string, data: string) -> string  HMAC-SHA256 (raw bytes)
  random_bytes(n: int) -> string             Crypto-random bytes
  const_eq(a: string, b: string) -> int      Constant-time comparison
  crypto_eq(a: string, b: string) -> int     Constant-time byte comparison
  secret_from_str(s: string) -> Secret       Wrap as secret (zeroized on drop)
  secret_drop(s: Secret) -> void             Securely erase
  aes_gcm_seal(key: string, nonce: string, pt: string, aad: string) -> string
  aes_gcm_open(key: string, nonce: string, ct: string, aad: string) -> string
  chacha20_poly1305_seal(key: string, nonce: string, pt: string, aad: string) -> string
  chacha20_poly1305_open(key: string, nonce: string, ct: string, aad: string) -> string

  // pull "crypto"  — package wrappers over the builtins above
  crypto.password_hash(password) -> string           Argon2id PHC (prefer for storage)
  crypto.password_verify(phc, password) -> int       1/0 constant-time verify
  crypto.bcrypt(password, cost) -> string            $2b$ (libxcrypt; crypto.bcrypt_ok)
  crypto.bcrypt_check(hash, password) -> int
  crypto.pbkdf2(password, salt, iterations, dklen) -> string  PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 raw

  // SCRAM-SHA-256 core (RFC 5802 / 7677) — compose AuthMessage yourself:
  //   auth = client_first_bare + "," + server_first + "," + client_final_no_proof
  // salt is raw bytes (base64-decode wire values first). See scram_test.mko.
  crypto.scram_salted_password(password, salt, iterations) -> string  // PBKDF2, 32 B
  crypto.scram_client_key(salted) -> string           // HMAC(salted, "Client Key")
  crypto.scram_server_key(salted) -> string           // HMAC(salted, "Server Key")
  crypto.scram_stored_key(client_key) -> string       // SHA256(client_key)
  crypto.scram_client_signature(stored_key, auth) -> string
  crypto.scram_server_signature(server_key, auth) -> string
  crypto.scram_client_proof(client_key, client_sig) -> string  // XOR
  crypto.scram_verify_proof(stored_key, auth, proof) -> int    // 1/0 (const_eq)

  // Hex helpers:
  hex_encode(sha256(data))                   // sha256_hex equivalent

  // JWT:
  jwt_sign(payload: string, secret: string) -> string
  jwt_verify(token: string, secret: string) -> int
  jwt_payload(token: string) -> string

  // XOR utility:
  xor_bytes(a: string, b: string) -> string  Byte-wise XOR

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.15 Encoding
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  base64_encode(data: string) -> string
  base64_decode(data: string) -> string
  base32_encode(data: string) -> string
  hex_encode(data: string) -> string
  hex_decode(data: string) -> string
  url_query_escape(s: string) -> string
  gzip_compress(data: string) -> string
  gzip_decompress(data: string) -> string

  // Binary encoding:
  binary_put_u16le(n: int) -> string
  binary_put_u32le(n: int) -> string
  binary_put_u64le(n: int) -> string
  binary_u16le(data: string) -> int
  binary_u32le(data: string) -> int
  binary_put_u16be(n: int) -> string
  binary_put_u32be(n: int) -> string
  binary_u16be(data: string) -> int
  binary_u32be(data: string) -> int

  // Serialization:
  yaml_get_string(yaml: string, key: string) -> string
  toml_get_string(toml: string, key: string) -> string
  toml_get_int(toml: string, key: string) -> int
  csv_split_line(line: string) -> []string
  csv_join_row(fields: []string) -> string
  xml_escape(s: string) -> string
  html_escape(s: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.16 Regular Expressions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  regex_match(pattern: string, s: string) -> bool      Full match
  regex_find(pattern: string, s: string) -> string      First match
  regex_find_all(pattern: string, s: string, max: int) -> []string
  regex_replace(pattern: string, s: string, repl: string) -> string
  regex_replace_all(pattern: string, s: string, repl: string) -> string
  regex_capture(pattern: string, s: string, group: int) -> string
  regex_valid(pattern: string) -> int
  regex_quote_meta(s: string) -> string

  // Supported: literals, ., X*, X+, X?, |, [abc], [a-z], [^...], (...), ^, $
  // Unicode: \p{L}, \p{N}, etc.
  // Backrefs: \1-\9

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.17 UUID and ULID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Uuid is a 16-byte value type. ULIDs use the same layout.

  uuid_v4() -> Uuid
  uuid_v5(ns: Uuid, name: string) -> Uuid     // SHA-1 name uuid
  uuid_v7() -> Uuid                           // time-ordered
  uuid_nil() -> Uuid
  uuid_ns_dns() / uuid_ns_url() / uuid_ns_oid() / uuid_ns_x500() -> Uuid
  uuid_string(u: Uuid) -> string              // lowercase 8-4-4-4-12
  uuid_string_upper(u: Uuid) -> string
  uuid_urn(u: Uuid) -> string
  uuid_bytes(u: Uuid) -> string               // raw 16 bytes
  uuid_from_bytes(s: string) -> Uuid          // aborts if len != 16
  uuid_parse(s: string) -> Uuid
  uuid_parse_ok(s: string) -> bool
  uuid_check(s: string) -> Result[int, string]
  uuid_eq(a: Uuid, b: Uuid) -> bool
  uuid_cmp(a: Uuid, b: Uuid) -> int           // -1, 0, 1
  uuid_is_nil(u: Uuid) -> bool
  uuid_version(u: Uuid) -> int
  uuid_variant(u: Uuid) -> int

  ulid_new() -> Uuid
  ulid_string(u: Uuid) -> string              // 26-char Crockford base32
  ulid_parse(s: string) -> Uuid
  ulid_parse_ok(s: string) -> bool
  ulid_timestamp_ms(u: Uuid) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.18 Cookies, Sessions & Auth
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Cookies:
  cookie_get(header: string, name: string) -> string
  cookie_make(name: string, value: string, max_age: int) -> string

  // Sessions:
  session_id_new() -> string

  // CSRF:
  csrf_token() -> string
  csrf_check(token: string, expected: string) -> int

  // Bearer auth:
  auth_bearer(header: string) -> string
  auth_check_bearer(header: string, expected: string) -> int

  // Basic auth:
  auth_basic_header(user: string, pass: string) -> string
  auth_check_basic(header: string, user: string, pass: string) -> int

  // Signed tokens (HMAC-SHA256):
  auth_token_sign(subject: string, secret: string) -> string
  auth_token_check(token: string, secret: string) -> int
  auth_token_subject(token: string) -> string

  // Role-based access:
  auth_role_has(roles: string, role: string) -> int
  authz_allow_role(user_roles: string, required: string) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.19 Channels
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  chan_new(capacity: int) -> chan[int]         Create buffered channel
  chan_open[int](capacity: int) -> chan[int]   Typed channel (int)
  chan_open[string](capacity: int) -> chan[string]  Typed channel (string)
  chan_open[bool](capacity: int) -> chan[bool] Typed channel (bool)
  chan_try_send(ch: chan[int], val: int) -> int  Non-blocking send (0=ok)
  chan_len(ch: chan[T]) -> int                 Items in channel (any T)
  chan_cap(ch: chan[T]) -> int                 Channel capacity (any T)
  chan_select2(a: chan[int], b: chan[int], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_select3(a: chan[int], b: chan[int], c: chan[int], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_str_select2(a: chan[string], b: chan[string], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_select_value() -> int

  // Move a string into a chan[string] without cloning. ch.send still clones.
  // try_send_take always consumes v (frees it if the send fails).
  chan_str_send_take(ch: chan[string], v: string) -> int      // 1 ok, 0 closed
  chan_str_try_send_take(ch: chan[string], v: string) -> int  // 1 queued, else 0

  ch.send(val)
  ch.recv()
  ch.close()

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.20 Concurrency Primitives
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Mutex:
  mutex_new() -> Mutex
  mutex_lock(m: Mutex) -> void
  mutex_unlock(m: Mutex) -> void

  // RWMutex:
  rwmutex_new() -> RWMutex
  rwmutex_rlock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_runlock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_lock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_unlock(m: RWMutex) -> void

  // Atomics:
  atomic_new(val: int) -> AtomicInt
  atomic_load(a: AtomicInt) -> int
  atomic_store(a: AtomicInt, val: int) -> void
  atomic_add(a: AtomicInt, delta: int) -> int
  atomic_cas(a: AtomicInt, expected: int, desired: int) -> int

  // WaitGroup:
  wait_group_new() -> WaitGroup
  wait_group_add(wg: WaitGroup, delta: int) -> void
  wait_group_done(wg: WaitGroup) -> void
  wait_group_wait(wg: WaitGroup) -> void

  // Shared references:
  share_int(val: int) -> ShareInt
  share_clone(s: ShareInt) -> ShareInt
  share_get(s: ShareInt) -> int
  share_set(s: ShareInt, val: int) -> void
  share_drop(s: ShareInt) -> void

  // Concurrent map:
  cmap_new() -> CMap
  cmap_set(m: CMap, key: string, val: string) -> void
  cmap_get(m: CMap, key: string) -> string
  cmap_has(m: CMap, key: string) -> int
  cmap_del(m: CMap, key: string) -> int
  cmap_len(m: CMap) -> int
  cmap_incr(m: CMap, key: string, delta: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.21 Signal Handling
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  signal_watch(name: string) -> int           Register signal handler (HUP/TERM/INT/USR1/USR2)
  signal_fired(name: string) -> int           Check if signal was received (1=yes, resets)
  signal_ignore(name: string) -> int          Ignore a signal

  // Example: graceful shutdown
  fn main() {
      signal_watch("TERM")
      signal_watch("HUP")

      while true {
          if signal_fired("TERM") == 1 {
              print("shutting down")
              break
          }
          if signal_fired("HUP") == 1 {
              print("reloading config")
          }
          sleep_ms(100)
      }
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.22 File-System Watch
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  watch_new() -> Watch                        Create watcher (kqueue on macOS, inotify on Linux)
  watch_add(w: Watch, path: string) -> int    Watch a file/directory for changes
  watch_poll(w: Watch, timeout_ms: int) -> string  Poll for event (returns path or "")
  watch_close(w: Watch) -> int                Close watcher

  // Example: hot reload
  fn main() {
      let w = watch_new()
      watch_add(w, "./config.toml")

      while true {
          let changed = watch_poll(w, 1000)
          if str_len(changed) > 0 {
              print("file changed: " + changed)
              // reload config
          }
      }
      watch_close(w)
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.23 Event Loop
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  evloop_new() -> EvLoop
  evloop_add(el: EvLoop, fd: int, flags: int) -> int
  evloop_mod(el: EvLoop, fd: int, flags: int) -> int
  evloop_del(el: EvLoop, fd: int) -> int
  evloop_wait(el: EvLoop, timeout_ms: int) -> int
  evloop_event_fd(el: EvLoop, idx: int) -> int
  evloop_event_flags(el: EvLoop, idx: int) -> int
  evloop_close(el: EvLoop) -> int

  // Non-blocking I/O:
  nb_listen(port: int) -> int
  nb_accept(fd: int) -> int
  nb_read(fd: int) -> string
  nb_write(fd: int, data: string) -> int
  nb_udp_bind(port: int) -> int
  nb_udp_recv(fd: int) -> string
  nb_close(fd: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.24 Binary Buffer
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  buf_pack_new(capacity: int) -> Buf
  buf_from_string(s: string) -> Buf
  buf_to_string(b: Buf) -> string
  buf_len(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_pos(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_reset(b: Buf) -> void
  buf_seek(b: Buf, pos: int) -> void
  buf_free(b: Buf) -> void

  // Write (little-endian unless noted):
  buf_write_u8(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u16(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u32(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u64(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_i32(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_f32(b: Buf, val: float) -> void
  buf_write_f64(b: Buf, val: float) -> void
  buf_write_bytes(b: Buf, data: string) -> void
  buf_write_str(b: Buf, s: string) -> void
  buf_write_u16be(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u32be(b: Buf, val: int) -> void

  // Read:
  buf_read_u8(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u16(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u32(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u64(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_i32(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_f32(b: Buf) -> float
  buf_read_f64(b: Buf) -> float
  buf_read_bytes(b: Buf, n: int) -> string
  buf_read_str(b: Buf) -> string
  buf_read_u16be(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u32be(b: Buf) -> int

  // Byte buffer pool:
  buf_get(size: int) -> []byte
  buf_put(b: []byte) -> void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.25 TCP & UDP Networking
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // TCP:
  tcp_listen(port: int) -> int
  tcp_listen_addr(addr: string, port: int) -> int   Bind to specific address
  tcp_accept(listener: int) -> int
  tcp_connect(host: string, port: int) -> int
  tcp_write(conn: int, data: string) -> int
  tcp_read(conn: int) -> string
  tcp_nodelay(conn: int) -> int
  tcp_close(conn: int) -> int

  // UDP:
  udp_bind(port: int) -> int
  udp_send_to(fd: int, host: string, port: int, data: string) -> int
  udp_recv(fd: int, max_bytes: int) -> string
  udp_close(fd: int) -> int

  // UDP proxy:
  game_udp_sender_addr(sock: GameUDP) -> string      Sender address
  game_udp_send_to(sock: GameUDP, addr: string, data: string) -> int

  // Unix sockets:
  unix_socket_pair() -> int
  unix_write(fd: int, data: string) -> int
  unix_read(fd: int, max: int) -> string
  unix_close(fd: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.26 TLS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // TLS 1.3 server (socket-style):
  tls_server_new(cert: string, key: string) -> TlsServer
  tls_accept(srv: TlsServer, fd: int) -> TlsConn
  tls_read(conn: TlsConn) -> string
  tls_write(conn: TlsConn, data: string) -> int
  tls_alpn(conn: TlsConn) -> string         Negotiated ALPN protocol

  // Legacy TLS server:
  tls_serve(port: int, cert: string, key: string, handler: string) -> int
  tls_serve_once(port: int, cert: string, key: string, response: string) -> int
  tls_serve_n(port: int, cert: string, key: string, response: string, n: int) -> int

  // TLS client:
  tls_get(host: string, port: int, path: string, ca: string) -> string
  tls_post(host: string, port: int, path: string, ca: string, body: string) -> string
  tls_get_insecure(host: string, port: int, path: string) -> string
  tls_handshake_ok(host: string, port: int, ca: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.27 Game Networking
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  game_udp_bind(port: int) -> GameUDP
  game_udp_recv(sock: GameUDP) -> string
  game_udp_sender(sock: GameUDP) -> int
  game_udp_send(sock: GameUDP, peer: int, data: string) -> int
  game_udp_broadcast(sock: GameUDP, data: string) -> int
  game_udp_kick(sock: GameUDP, peer: int) -> void
  game_udp_peers(sock: GameUDP) -> int
  game_udp_fd(sock: GameUDP) -> int
  game_udp_close(sock: GameUDP) -> void
  tick_now_us() -> int
  tick_sleep_us(target: int, period: int) -> int

  // Fixed timestep:
  game_fixed_steps(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int
  game_fixed_remainder(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int
  game_alpha(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int

  // Fixed-point math:
  fx_from_int(val: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_to_int(val: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_mul(a: int, b: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_div(a: int, b: int, shift: int) -> int

  // Deterministic RNG:
  det_rng_next(state: int) -> int
  det_rng_range(state: int, max: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.28 Cloud / Distributed
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Consistent hashing:
  chash_new(replicas: int, nodes: int) -> CHash
  chash_get(ring: CHash, key: string) -> int
  chash_add_node(ring: CHash) -> int
  chash_remove_node(ring: CHash, node: int) -> void
  chash_node_count(ring: CHash) -> int
  chash_free(ring: CHash) -> void

  // Rate limiting:
  ratelimit_new(max: int, window_ms: int) -> RateLimiter
  ratelimit_allow(rl: RateLimiter) -> int
  ratelimit_remaining(rl: RateLimiter) -> int
  ratelimit_free(rl: RateLimiter) -> void
  rate_allow(key: string, max: int, window_ms: int) -> int

  // Circuit breaker:
  breaker_new(threshold: int, timeout_ms: int, half_max: int) -> CircuitBreaker
  breaker_allow(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> int
  breaker_success(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_failure(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_state(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> int    // 0=closed, 1=open, 2=half-open
  breaker_reset(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_free(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void

  // Backoff:
  backoff_ms(attempt: int, base_ms: int, max_ms: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.29 Testing
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  assert(cond: bool) -> void
  assert_eq(a: int, b: int) -> void
  assert_eq_str(a: string, b: string) -> void
  fail(msg: string) -> void
  t_run(name: string) -> void
  t_run_nested(name: string) -> void
  black_box(n: int) -> int

  // HTTP test helpers:
  httptest_serve_once(port: int, response: string) -> int
  httptest_get(url: string) -> string
  httptest_status() -> int
  httptest_header(name: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.30 Runtime
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  runtime_stats_json() -> string
  exit(code: int) -> void
  leak_mark() -> int
  leak_bytes_since(mark: int) -> int
  leak_detected(mark: int) -> int
  leak_check(mark: int) -> int                1 if leak detected since mark, else 0
  leak_report_json() -> string                JSON report of leaked allocations
  alloc_track_reset() -> int
  alloc_live_bytes() -> int
  alloc_high_bytes() -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.31 Logging
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  log_info(msg: string)
  log_warn(msg: string)
  log_error(msg: string)
  log_kv(level: string, key: string, val: string)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.32 Router & Middleware
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  router_new() -> int
  router_group(router: int, prefix: string) -> int
  router_add(router: int, method: string, pattern: string, handler: string) -> int
  router_match(router: int, req: HttpRequest) -> string
  router_param(router: int, req: HttpRequest, name: string) -> string

  reqctx_new() -> int
  reqctx_set(ctx: int, key: string, val: string) -> int
  reqctx_get(ctx: int, key: string) -> string
  middleware_allow_methods(req: HttpRequest, methods: string) -> int

================================================================================
26.33 Checked Arithmetic
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  checked_add(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Add with overflow check
  checked_sub(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Subtract with overflow check
  checked_mul(a: int, b: int) -> Result[int, string]  Multiply with overflow check
  would_overflow_add(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if addition would overflow
  would_overflow_sub(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if subtraction would overflow
  would_overflow_mul(a: int, b: int) -> int   1 if multiplication would overflow

  // Example:
  match checked_add(a, b) {
      Ok(sum) => print(sum),
      Err(e) => print("overflow: " + e),
  }
  if would_overflow_mul(x, y) == 1 {
      print("would overflow")
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.34 Graceful Shutdown
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  install_graceful_shutdown() -> void          Install SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers
  shutdown_requested() -> int                  1 if shutdown signal received, else 0

  // Example: HTTP server with graceful shutdown
  fn main() {
      install_graceful_shutdown()
      let fd = http_bind(8080)
      while shutdown_requested() == 0 {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let _ = http_respond(c, 200, "ok\n")
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
      let _ = http_close_listener(fd)
      print("shutting down")
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.35 Distributed Tracing
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  trace_begin(name: string) -> void           Start a trace span
  trace_end() -> void                         End the current span
  trace_id() -> string                        Current trace ID (hex string)
  trace_log(msg: string) -> void              Attach a log event to the current span

  // Example:
  fn handle_request(c: int) {
      trace_begin("handle_request")
      let tid = trace_id()
      // ... do work ...
      trace_log("processed request")
      trace_end()
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.36 Leak Detection
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  leak_mark() -> int                          Snapshot current allocation state
  leak_check(mark: int) -> int                1 if allocations leaked since mark
  leak_report_json() -> string                JSON report of leaked allocations

  // Example:
  fn main() {
      let mark = leak_mark()
      // ... allocate things ...
      if leak_check(mark) != 0 {
          print("leak detected: " + leak_report_json())
      }
  }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
26.37 Crew Drain
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  crew_drain(timeout_ms: int) -> int          Wait for all crew jobs, with timeout

  // Example:
  crew t {
      let j = t.kick(long_running_work())
      // drain with 5-second timeout
      crew_drain(5000)
  }

  crew_drain waits for all outstanding jobs in the enclosing crew block to
  complete, up to the specified timeout in milliseconds. Returns 0 if all
  jobs finished, 1 if the timeout elapsed.

================================================================================
27. COMMON PATTERNS
================================================================================

27.1 HTTP Server
----------------

  fn main() {
      let fd = http_bind(8080)
      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let method = http_method(c)
          let path = http_path(c)

          if path == "/health" {
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, "{\"ok\":true}\n")
          } else if path == "/api/users" and method == "POST" {
              let body = http_body(c)
              let name = json_get_string(body, "name")
              let resp = json_object("created", name)
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 201, resp)
          } else {
              let _ = http_respond(c, 404, "not found\n")
          }
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
  }

27.2 Error Handling Chain
-------------------------

  fn load_config(path: string) -> Result[string, string] {
      if not file_exists(path) {
          return error("config file not found")
      }
      let data = read_file(path)
      if str_len(data) == 0 {
          return error("config file is empty")
      }
      return Ok(data)
  }

  fn start() -> Result[int, string] {
      let cfg = wrap_err(load_config("app.toml"), "startup")?
      let port = parse_int(toml_get_string(cfg, "port"))?
      return Ok(port)
  }

27.3 Concurrent Workers
------------------------

  fn main() {
      let ch = chan_new(100)
      crew t {
          let w1 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w1"))
          let w2 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w2"))
          let w3 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w3"))
          let w4 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w4"))

          for i in range 100 {
              let _ = ch.send(i)
          }
          ch.close()

          let _ = w1.join()
          let _ = w2.join()
          let _ = w3.join()
          let _ = w4.join()
      }
  }

  fn worker(ch: chan[int], name: string) -> int {
      for v in range ch {
          print(name + ": processing " + format_int(v))
      }
      return 0
  }

27.4 Arena-Scoped Request Processing
-------------------------------------

  fn handle_request(c: int) {
      arena req {
          let mut parts = make([]string, 0, 16)
          let body = http_body(c)
          parts = append(parts, json_get_string(body, "name"))
          parts = append(parts, json_get_string(body, "email"))
          let resp = json_ss("name", parts[0], "email", parts[1])
          let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, resp)
      }
      // All arena memory freed here
      let _ = http_close(c)
  }

27.5 Fan with Crew for Parallel Processing
