-------------------------------------------

  fn process_batch(items: []int) -> []int {
      let results = fan(items, fn(item) { item * item + 1 })
      return results
  }

  fn main() {
      let data = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
      let out = process_batch(data)
      for v in out {
          print_int(v)
      }
  }

27.6 Methods and Generics
---------------------------

  export struct Stack {
      items: []int
      size: int
  }

  on Stack {
      fn push(self, val: int) -> Stack {
          return Stack { items: append(self.items, val), size: self.size + 1 }
      }

      fn pop(self) -> (int, Stack) {
          let top = self.items[self.size - 1]
          return (top, Stack { items: self.items[:self.size - 1], size: self.size - 1 })
      }

      fn peek(self) -> int {
          return self.items[self.size - 1]
      }
  }

  fn id[T](x: T) -> T {
      return x
  }

  fn main() {
      let s = Stack { items: make([]int, 0, 8), size: 0 }
      let s2 = s.push(10)
      let s3 = s2.push(20)
      print_int(s3.peek())           // 20

      let n = id(42)
      let msg = id("hello")
      print_int(n)
      print(msg)
  }

27.7 Switch and If-Expression
------------------------------

  fn status_text(code: int) -> string {
      switch code {
          case 200:
              return "OK"
          case 404:
              return "Not Found"
          case 500:
              return "Internal Server Error"
          default:
              return "Unknown"
      }
  }

  fn classify(n: int) -> string {
      let label = if n > 0 { "positive" } else { "non-positive" }
      return label
  }

27.8 Module System Example
----------------------------

  // math_lib.mko
  pack mathlib

  export fn square(n: int) -> int {
      return n * n
  }

  export fn cube(n: int) -> int {
      return n * n * n
  }

  fn helper() -> int {       // pack-private
      return 0
  }

  // main.mko
  pull "./math_lib.mko" as math

  fn main() {
      print_int(math.square(5))    // 25
      print_int(math.cube(3))      // 27
  }

27.9 Signal-Based Graceful Shutdown
------------------------------------

  fn main() {
      signal_watch("TERM")
      signal_watch("INT")

      let fd = http_bind(8080)
      while true {
          if signal_fired("TERM") == 1 or signal_fired("INT") == 1 {
              print("shutting down gracefully")
              http_close_listener(fd)
              break
          }
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let _ = http_respond(c, 200, "ok\n")
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
  }

27.10 File-System Watch Hot Reload
-----------------------------------

  fn main() {
      let w = watch_new()
      watch_add(w, "./config.toml")

      let mut config = read_file("./config.toml")
      print("loaded config")

      while true {
          let changed = watch_poll(w, 2000)
          if str_len(changed) > 0 {
              config = read_file("./config.toml")
              print("config reloaded")
          }
      }
      watch_close(w)
  }

27.11 TLS 1.3 Server
---------------------

  fn main() {
      let srv = tls_server_new("cert.pem", "key.pem")
      let listener = tcp_listen(8443)

      while true {
          let fd = tcp_accept(listener)
          if fd < 0 { continue }
          let conn = tls_accept(srv, fd)
          let req = tls_read(conn)
          tls_write(conn, "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\r\nhello tls\n")
      }
  }

27.12 Parallel Assignment and Compound Ops
--------------------------------------------

  fn fib_pair(n: int) -> (int, int) {
      let mut a = 0
      let mut b = 1
      for i in range n {
          a, b = b, a + b             // parallel swap
      }
      return (a, b)
  }

  fn main() {
      let mut counter = 0
      counter += 10
      counter++
      print_int(counter)              // 11

      let f, g = fib_pair(10)
      print_int(f)                    // 55
  }

27.13 Rate-Limited HTTP Server
-------------------------------

  fn main() {
      let rl = ratelimit_new(100, 1000)   // 100 req/sec
      let fd = http_bind(8080)

      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }

          if ratelimit_allow(rl) == 0 {
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 429, "{\"error\":\"rate limit exceeded\"}")
              let _ = http_close(c)
              continue
          }

          let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, "{\"ok\":true}")
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
      ratelimit_free(rl)
  }

27.14 Event Loop Server
------------------------

  fn main() {
      let el = evloop_new()
      let server_fd = nb_listen(8080)
      let _ = evloop_add(el, server_fd, 1)

      while true {
          let n = evloop_wait(el, 1000)
          let mut i = 0
          while i < n {
              let fd = evloop_event_fd(el, i)
              if fd == server_fd {
                  let client = nb_accept(server_fd)
                  if client >= 0 {
                      let _ = evloop_add(el, client, 1)
                  }
              } else {
                  let data = nb_read(fd)
                  let _ = nb_write(fd, "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\r\nhi\n")
                  let _ = evloop_del(el, fd)
                  let _ = nb_close(fd)
              }
              i++
          }
      }
      let _ = evloop_close(el)
  }

27.15 Binary Protocol
---------------------

  fn encode_message(msg_type: int, payload: string) -> string {
      let b = buf_pack_new(64)
      buf_write_u8(b, msg_type)
      buf_write_u32be(b, str_len(payload))
      buf_write_bytes(b, payload)
      let wire = buf_to_string(b)
      buf_free(b)
      return wire
  }

  fn decode_message(wire: string) -> string {
      let r = buf_from_string(wire)
      let msg_type = buf_read_u8(r)
      let msg_len = buf_read_u32be(r)
      let payload = buf_read_bytes(r, msg_len)
      buf_free(r)
      return payload
  }

================================================================================
28. KEYWORDS (COMPLETE LIST)
================================================================================

38 reserved words (always keywords, never identifiers):

  actor and arena as break const continue crew default defer else enum extern
  false fan fn for hold if import in interface join kick let match mut not or
  range receive return select share struct timeout true while

Contextual keywords (also usable as identifiers):
  pack pull switch go export case

NOT keywords (ordinary identifiers / builtins):
  Type names: int, int8, int32, int64, uint64, byte, float, float64, string
  Constructors: Ok, Err, Some, None
  Builtins: len, cap, append, copy, make, has, delete, assert, assert_eq, t_run
  All stdlib functions (print, str_len, etc.)

================================================================================
29. COMPLETE OPERATOR TABLE
================================================================================

  =                   Assignment (never equality)
  == != < > <= >=     Comparison
  + - * / %           Arithmetic
  += -= *= /=         Compound assignment
  ++ --               Increment / Decrement
  && || !             Logical (short-circuit)
  and or not          Logical (keyword form, same as above)
  & | ^ &^ << >>      Bitwise
  ^x                  Unary bitwise complement
  |params| { body }   Lambda (leading | is lambda, infix | is bitwise or)
  fn(params) { body } Lambda (block form)

================================================================================
30. TYPE CONVERSION TABLE
================================================================================

  int -> int64:       int64(x)
  int -> int32:       int32(x)
  int -> int8:        int8(x)        range-checked -128..127
  int -> uint64:      uint64(x)      rejects negative
  int -> byte:        byte(x)        range-checked 0..255
  int -> float:       float64(x)
  int -> string:      string(x)      decimal representation
  float -> int:       truncates
  string -> []byte:   bytes(s) or []byte(s)
  []byte -> string:   string(b)

================================================================================
31. DUAL FORMS (COMPAT SUGAR — NOT PREFERRED)
================================================================================

These still parse. Prefer the right-hand column in new code; mako fmt does too.

  Also accepted                     | Prefer
  ----------------------------------|---------------------------
  func f(a int) int                 | fn f(a: int) -> int
  func (p T) M() int                | on T { fn M(self) -> int }
  type T struct { x int }           | struct T { x: int }
  := / var                          | let / let mut
  package name                      | pack name
  import "path"                     | pull "path"
  import "path" as name             | pull "path" as name
  Capitalized = exported            | export fn / export struct

================================================================================
32. PERFORMANCE (runtime optimizations)
================================================================================

These are built in — no user action required.

Map hashing
  wyhash (8 bytes/iteration, 128-bit multiply mixing) replaces byte-at-a-time
  FNV-1a. 4-8x faster for string-keyed maps.

String interpolation
  f"..." uses a 256-byte stack buffer. Short interpolated strings (routes,
  log lines) never touch the allocator. Spills to heap only when needed.

Constant folding
  Integer binary expressions with literal operands (1 + 2, flags & 0xff)
  are folded at compile time. No runtime code emitted.

Zero-copy string comparisons
  x == "literal", str_eq(s, "literal"), str_has_prefix, str_has_suffix,
  str_contains, match arm string patterns, and print("literal") use
  mako_str_view (zero allocation, points into read-only data).

Channel select
  select waits on a shared condition variable. send/close broadcast wakeups
  (not 2ms nanosleep polling). 50ms max wait slice for edge races.

HTTP connection tracking
  Active connections tracked via atomic counter (O(1) instead of O(n) scan).
  Connection table supports up to 1024 concurrent connections.

HTTP header interning
  Header name lookup dispatches by length (switch), skipping non-matching
  headers without any string comparison.

Codegen allocator pressure
  Hot emission paths use format_args! into the output buffer directly,
  avoiding per-line String heap allocation. Map monomorph checks use a
  joined key set instead of per-call String pair allocation.

================================================================================
33. GOTCHAS (runtime)
================================================================================

HTTP/2 DATA size
  Peers reject DATA frames over their max frame size (default 16384). Use
  http2_data_frame or the TLS H2 reply helpers; they split for you. A single
  oversized frame shows up in Chrome as net::ERR_HTTP2_FRAME_SIZE_ERROR.

Empty string and free
  "" is a singleton. Do not free it with raw free(). The runtime uses
  mako_str_free for that. This bites HTTP, proxy, maps, zip, and reflect code
  if you free field data by hand.

Edge ALPN
  Some edges (e.g. mako-lang.com) stay on http/1.1 until multi-stream H2 is
  solid. If you turn h2 back on, use a build that splits DATA frames.

================================================================================
34. END
================================================================================

# https://mako-lang.com
# https://github.com/loreste/mako
# 0.2.1
